title: dryopteris × uliginosa
intro: Dryopteris × uliginosa, also known as the bog fern or uliginosum x dryopteris fern, is a natural hybrid species resulting from the cross between Dryopteris uliginosa and Dryopteris carthusiana, which are both types of woodland ferns. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris × uliginosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate congestion, and soothe respiratory tract irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the dried fronds being used in traditional medicine to create remedies for respiratory issues.
